questions: Should the structured backfill parity report live under `reports/`, or is there an existing project-preferred artifact directory for validation outputs?
When expiring stale leases, should old running rows be marked `failed` or `skipped`? I will default to `failed` because a crashed runner is an abnormal termination.

success_criteria.criterion: The two `20260513230500_live_update_topic_editor_phase1.sql` migration copies are updated identically for `tool_call_sequence`, revised transition uniqueness, and stale lease expiry support.
A create/watch tool call with collision override persists both the accepted transition and override audit transition for the same Anthropic `tool_use_id` without violating uniqueness.
Replaying the same write tool call after a simulated process restart returns the prior DB-stored result and performs no duplicate topic/source/transition side effects.
Lease acquisition steals only stale running leases older than the configured timeout and still rejects active non-expired concurrent leases.
`TOPIC_EDITOR_LEASE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` controls the lease timeout and defaults to 30 minutes when unset.
Prod backfill parity is run in dry-run/read-only mode and a structured report records legacy feed/watchlist counts versus generated posted/watching topic counts and mismatches.
No changes are made to `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` or `src/features/summarising/live_update_prompts.py`.
Existing focused topic-editor tests remain green after the patch.
The idempotency implementation keeps one canonical replay row per write tool call while allowing extra audit rows for distinct events such as overrides.
Backfill report output is easy for a reviewer to diff or parse without reading terminal logs.

assumptions: The redesign doc’s audit-trail requirement is authoritative, so the plan uses a sequence column rather than updating a rejected row in place.
The canonical idempotency result row is the transition with `tool_call_sequence = 0`; override rows use positive sequence values and are audit-only for replay purposes.
The prod Supabase credentials needed for the dry-run parity command are available in the local environment or `.env`.
Marking expired crashed leases as `failed` is acceptable unless the user prefers `skipped`.
Read-only tool calls do not need persisted replay because they have no write side effects and the explicit issue is double-execution of write tools.
